Name: Custom banned password protection not in use
Description:
This indicator checks if custom banned password protection is enabled in Entra ID.

Required permissions

  • Directory.Read.All
LikelihoodOfCompromise:
Custom banned password protection is a feature of Entra password protection that allows organizations to specify custom banned words, helping to prevent users from creating weak passwords that may contain words relative to the organization.

Attackers often use password guessing attacks to take control over user accounts. Targeted password attacks on organizations may include passwords containing words relative to the organization, such as the company name, products or services offered, locality references, and others.

References

Configure custom Microsoft Entra password protection lists - Microsoft Entra ID | Microsoft Learn

Password protection in Microsoft Entra ID - Microsoft Entra ID | Microsoft Learn
ResultMessage: Custom banned passwords not in use.
Remediation:
Enable Enforce custom list in Entra ID and populate the Custom banned password list.

To enable, open the Microsoft Entra admin center (direct link), expand Protection and select Authentication methods.

In the blade on the right, select Password protection.

Set Enforce custom list to Enabled if it is not already enabled.

Enter custom banned words.

Organizations should also routinely verify the list and make additions or changes as needed.
